evidence_quotes.quote: Gamers invest time and money(!) into games, they purchase(!) them; protecting them from being deleted safeguards their purchases(!).

paragraphs.text: I will keep it short because time is valuable: 1. Video games are an important part of contemporary culture and art preserving them ensures future generations can experience and learn from them, as a cultural artifact, exactly likes books, movies, and music. 2. Gamers invest time and money(!) into games, they purchase(!) them; protecting them from being deleted safeguards their purchases(!). 3. Direct access to older games can support developers, historians, and enthusiasts in studying past designs & stories, learning from good implementations, inspiring new ideas and improvements. 4. The most personal reason is the ability to learn interactively. As I grew up sick I spent a lot of time in-doors learning history, strategy, math, science, psychology, languages, organizational skills, and so much more - all through videogames! Thank you for reading!
